title: Philosophy Foundations
description: Learn and master the concepts of Philosophy Foundations with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Logic
description: This course develops **formal reasoning skills**, making it one of the most valuable courses in the entire Philosophy curriculum.

title: Epistemology
description: This course explores **how we know what we know**, one of the central questions of philosophy.

title: Metaphysics
description: This course explores **the fundamental nature of reality and existence**, asking what kinds of things exist and how they relate to one another.

title: Ethics
description: This course explores **how we should act, what makes actions right or wrong, and how ethical principles apply to modern society.**

title: Political Philosophy
description: This course explores **how societies should be organized and governed, and the philosophical foundations of political systems.**

title: Philosophy of Mind
description: This course explores **the nature of the mind, consciousness, and the relationship between mental processes and the physical brain.**

title: Philosophy of Science
description: This course explores **the foundations, methods, and limits of scientific knowledge.**

title: Philosophy of Language
description: This course explores **how language conveys meaning, shapes thought, and enables communication.**

title: Aesthetics
description: This course explores **the philosophy of beauty, art, creativity, and aesthetic experience.**

title: History of Philosophy
description: This course traces **the evolution of philosophical thought across civilizations and historical periods.**

title: Contemporary Philosophy
description: This capstone course explores **the major philosophical movements and challenges shaping the modern world.**
